title: Which do you prefer more
wikitext:
[]: Which do you prefer more...             Quote Post by bap_120790 » Mon Nov 19, 2007 2:30 pm The manga or anime? For me, I love the manga more, first because of the better drawing, and then for its details/more indepth events. Although the anime's excellent as well as we all know.

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Mon Nov 19, 2007 7:32 pm Why ask the obvious questions? Why not? Anime, anime, anime. The manga might in some respects "have it all together", but it's got manga-Shinji, which is automatic fail. Besides, the messy nature of the Evangelion anime is part of the fun. Strangely, a lot of non-Eva fans seem to prefer the manga, maybe for reasons that I don't. *note: Point should not be considered valid since I only read two volumes of the manga and have only been keeping up with some details*

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 19, 2007 7:46 pm obviously, the anime you cant hear Shinji scream his ass of in the Manga not even the power of *IMAGINATION!* can do that

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 19, 2007 7:49 pm Anime for yours truly. A few reasons off the top of my head on why (aka Epic Manga-Ranting ahead!): 1) The anime was actually first, and IMO, the better of the two mediums. (Though I only own Vol.1 of the manga and have read a few volumes online & at bookstores.) 2) The characters personalties and psychological aspects are portrayed MUCH more better in the anime. To be frank, I could care less that the manga is Rei/Shinji, but the fact that Sadamoto flat-out ignores Shinji's other relationships (Misato & Asuka for instance) as well as totally changing Shinji's personality from an insecure nervous kid to a violent smart-mouthed prick does NOT sit well with me. 3) Despite the fact Shinji himself in an early volume said something about not liking Rei romantically but instead feeling that she's "an important part of him that was taken away", it sure as hell seems as a romantic interest on either side to me! Can you say poor writing? Also, if Sadamoto decides to take the actual EoE ending route instead of something like Last B served arm ending, that was just be poor writing right there. (Asuka & Shinji don't even at least seem to be friends in the manga; let alone interested in each other romantically, simply room-mates/co-workers. :roll: ) 4) The mood doesn't come across the same in the manga. Especially the psychological aspects of the series. 5) Lack of the greatness that is Ogata Megumi (and her kick-ass vocal range of screaming) among many other seiyuu. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Nov 19, 2007 7:51 pm "Manga Asuka" is nothing like "*cough*real*cough* Anime Asuka", and her entire psychology is changed. I like Asuka because she's got real-life emotional childhood trauma and has reacted to it by becoming extroverted; manga-Asuka is a test tube baby. Right....

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:01 pm The anime, but only because I don't ever read manga. I can't read or write.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:02 pm @ V: I forgot about that point. Makes no sense... @ Brik: So how are you in Med School then? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:06 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: @ Brik: So how are you in Med School then? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:14 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: @ V: I forgot about that point. Makes no sense... @ Brik: So how are you in Med School then? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:15 pm I think he meant Japanese conji. Anime, because I can't read or write Japanese conji. And I don't like squinting, or black and white comics, unless they're REALLY good.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:16 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: I think he meant Japanese Kanji.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:26 pm I like the anime better in all respects except one. Touji/Hikari was better developed in the manga. Touji dying is far more dramatic. But that's completely offset by EMK and his kitten killing as well as Manga Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 19, 2007 8:28 pm Shiro wrote: Touji/Hikari was better developed in the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:08 pm @ Sachi Thanx. I don't know Kanji =)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:27 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: I think he meant Japanese conji.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Nov 19, 2007 9:28 pm If you weren't sooo sarcastic I might actually believe you.

[]: Re: Which do you prefer more...             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Nov 19, 2007 10:18 pm bap_120790 wrote: The manga or anime? For me, I love the manga more, first because of the better drawing, and then for its details/more indepth events. Although the anime's excellent as well as we all know.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Mon Nov 19, 2007 10:24 pm The manga has it's moments (Kaji's back story for instance), but I prefer movies and television over reading. So naturally I'm going to to have a preference for the anime. As a wise man once said: Books are all fact; no heart.

[]: Re: Which do you prefer more...             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Nov 19, 2007 10:26 pm Alaska Slim wrote: For myself, of course, the anime. Manga Shinji is a stereotype, one I can stand even less than the original one, not to mention in the manga both his and Asuka's pasts are screwed around with, making their problems more 'explainable' and 'simplified', something that cheapens them overall.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Nov 19, 2007 10:33 pm ^ The man speakth the truth for once!

[]:             Quote Post by Incisivis » Mon Nov 19, 2007 7:32 pm Why ask the obvious questions?
